LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-26-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RICHARD P.'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS LODGED WITH PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS LODGED WITH PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 2.550, SUBDIVISION (D) AS THE DOCUMENTS CONTAIN PERSONAL HEALTH INFORMATION SUBJECT TO HIPAA AND A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE FOLLOWING DOCUMENTS ARE SEALED: (1) THE UNREDACTED DECLARATION OF DAVID M. LILIENTSTEIN IN SUPPORT OF RICHARD P.'S RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND EXHIBITS A-B, (2) THE UNREDACTED VERSION OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, AND (3) THE UNREDACTED VERSION OF PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT AND SUPPORTING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH MUST RECITE THIS COURT'S FINDINGS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 2.550, SUBDIVISION (E). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Continued To Aug-23-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2021 Off Calendar

Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Lodged
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE's Motion To Seal Documents Lodged.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Grant. No opposition filed, and the Court finds that the requirements of Cal. R. Ct. 2.550 are met. Defendant shall prepare an order identifying the documents to be sealed and set forth the following findings: (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Plaintiff Richard P.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Lodged With Plaintiffs Opposition To Defendants Motion For Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. KATIE J. SPIELMAN, Esq. and DAVID M. LILIENSTEIN, Esq. of DL Law Group (Tel: 415-678-5050) for plaintiff Richard P.;
Atty. KATE M. HAMMOND, Esq. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP (Tel: 310-312-4000) for defendant California Physicians' Services dba Blue Shield of California, erroneously sued as Blue Shield of California.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RICHARD P.'s Motion To Seal Documents Lodged With Plaintiff's Opposition To Defendants Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Motion to Seal Documents Lodged with Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted pursuant to California Rules of Court, rule 2.550, subdivision (d) as the documents contain personal health information subject to HIPAA and a stipulated protective order. No opposition filed. The Court orders that the following documents are sealed: (1) the Unredacted Declaration of David M. Lilientstein in Support of Richard P.'s Response to Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication and Exhibits A-B, (2) the unredacted version of Plaintiff's Opposition to Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication, and (3) the unredacted version of Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Separate Statement and Supporting Evidence.

Plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which must recite this Court's findings in compliance with California Rules of Court, rule 2.550, subdivision (e).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Defendant California Physicians Service Dba Blue Shield Of Californias Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Lodged With Its Motion For Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE dba Blue Shield Of California's Motion To Seal Documents Lodged With Its Motion For Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion granted. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
